name: Gallo-Romance Vulgar Latin

ancestors.notes: Gaulish substrate: vigesimal counting, u-fronting [u]>[y], lexicon (briser, chemin, char). Heaviest Celtic substrate in any Romance language.
Early Germanic superstrate (Visigoths, Burgundians in S. Gaul; Franks in N. Gaul). Lexicon: blanc, guerre, jardin.

notes: Gallo-Romance Vulgar Latin (~3rd–7th c. CE). Spoken Latin of Gaul and NE Hispania. Ancestral to French, Occitan, Catalan, Franco-Provençal. Key innovations vs Ibero-Romance: (1) /u/ → /y/ fronting (mūrum → [myːr]). (2) More aggressive lenition (intervocalic stops → ∅). (3) CA- → [tʃ] palatalisation. (4) F- PRESERVED (not → [h]). (5) Nasal vowels develop. (6) Final vowel loss more extreme. (7) Voiced /v/ preserved (not merged with /b/). Gaulish substrate is the strongest Celtic substrate in any Romance language (Price 1971, Bec 1970).

sources.author: Lloyd, P.M.
sources.title: From Latin to Spanish, vol. I

sources.author: Weiss, M.
sources.title: Outline of the Historical and Comparative Grammar of Latin

sources.author: Allen, W.S.
sources.title: Vox Latina: A Guide to the Pronunciation of Classical Latin
